title: Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual
caption: In 2023, the composition of religious and spiritual identity varies significantly across 25 countries, as shown by a stacked horizontal bar chart. India leads with the highest share of people identifying as "Religious" (dark blue), accounting for roughly 65% of the population, followed by Nigeria and Turkey, where this category also dominates. In contrast, Japan and Australia show minimal shares in the "Religious" category, with Japan at under 5% and Australia at around 10%. The "Both religious and spiritual" group (light green) is most prominent in Tanzania and the Philippines, each exceeding 30%, while it is nearly absent in Egypt and Brazil. The "Spiritual" category (dark green) peaks in Mexico and the United States, both hovering near 40%, whereas it is negligible in India and Nigeria. The "Neither religious nor spiritual" segment (pink) is largest in Sweden, Spain, and the United Kingdom, each surpassing 50%, and smallest in India and Nigeria. The "Did not answer/'I don't know'" portion (gray) remains consistently small across all nations, rarely exceeding 5%. The most visible pattern is the inverse relationship between the "Religious" and "Neither religious nor spiritual" categories: countries with high shares in one tend to have low shares in the other.
